manifest: Fractera agents do not deliver an automation in final form straight from a request — they build a platform for developing repeatable automations: standardized reuse, a visual interface, input/output data in the local DB and vector memory, fast switching from the user UI. Fractera lets Hermes and a coding agent build a finished-cycle tool — an n8n for one single task: the user does not recreate the task, they open it in the UI, run it and track the result.
about: Starter interface of ONE project of the Projects layer, shaped by the result contract (R9, step 184): a description block (why / what is automated / how it works), an interactive react-flow process diagram driven by declarative data (node click opens the full info panel — kind, task, processes, tools, env keys, io, connections — R8), an admin run panel that starts the durable workflow with an optional input, a processes table and a results table (the runs dashboard), and a scheduled-runs table read from the co-located cron.json. Mounted OUTSIDE [lang] into the monolingual, architect+manager-gated projects zone — access and language are INHERITED from the zone layout (app/(projects)/layout.tsx), so this primitive carries NO role/i18n seams. Composed by file copy + token substitution — no code generation.

envelope.zone: app/(projects)/projects — the category folder must already exist in the slot
envelope.rendering: dynamic (the zone role gate reads cookies — the sanctioned cockpit exception to static-first)
envelope.roles: architect+manager, inherited from the zone layout — not injectable, not overridable
envelope.i18n: mono (the site's default language, inherited from the zone layout)

seams.diagram-as-data: The react-flow diagram is rendered by one fixed client component; its shape lives in _data/flow.ts (plain nodes/edges + per-node info: summary, processes, kind, task, tools, envKeys, io — the full node payload of the info panel, R8). Nodes are movable (edges follow) and every node opens the info panel, but the canvas can never create or delete nodes/edges. Under contract R6 the diagram is the project's ONLY execution schema — what is not on it does not exist in the project; a decomposed project GETS this file generated from the graph by orchestrate-project-by-steps (deterministic rewrite on every approved run), so finishing the diagram = extending the GRAPH; a hand-composed project edits the data directly — either way a coding-agent handoff, never a template change.
seams.run-panel: run-panel.client.tsx is the admin launch panel (R9): a textarea for the optional run input + a Run button that POSTs to /api/projects/<category>/<project>/run from the signed-in (architect/manager) browser session and reports the started runId via a toast. The run journals itself into project_cron_runs, so it shows up in the page tables on the next load.
seams.tables-p6: _lib/project-data.ts reads project_cron_runs from the shared app DB (filtered by this category/project) — rows are written by the substrate cron runner (fractera-cron) and by the durable workflow (created_by='wdk'). Declaring jobs for the project = a cron.json next to the page; the runner picks it up within a tick, no restart. getCronJobs() reads that same cron.json (plain file read) to render the scheduled-runs queue table (R9).
seams.workflow-as-execution: app/api/projects/<category>/<project>/_workflow/definition.ts is the durable Workflow DevKit function — it lives next to its run route under app/api/, NOT in the page folder (WDK derives the workflow name from the file path and forbids the parentheses of route groups like (projects)) — the EXECUTABLE mirror of the diagram data (contract R6: diagram and workflow are ISOMORPHIC; the placeholder steps work/store/publish correspond to the starter flow nodes; the trigger node is the run route). The composed placeholder carries a fractera:starter-workflow marker: a decomposed project gets this file REPLACED by orchestrate-project-by-steps with a skeleton generated from the graph — one 'use step' per non-trigger node, each under a // node:<id> marker the engine validates on every run; the coder implements ONLY the step bodies and keeps the markers. fractera-cron stays the ONLY scheduler: a cron.json http action posts to /api/projects/<category>/<project>/run, which start()s the workflow; WDK's own scheduling is not used. The workflow journals its run into project_cron_runs (created_by='wdk') through the slot's db layer, so runs appear in the page tables with no page changes. Finishing the workflow for the real project = editing the step bodies — a coding-agent handoff, never a template change.
seams.declaration: README.md carries the /service/architecture machine block (fractera:meta) with visibility/roles + cron/integrations, so the composed project round-trips through the declare panel.
seams.hooks: The Hooks layer (step 187.4) is a native part of the primitive: _data/hooks.ts carries the project's DEFAULT_HOOKS (seeded at compose time from {{PROJECT_HOOK_PHRASES}} — the 82-lang default-hook-phrases catalog for the slot language). hooks-panel.client.tsx (mounted in index when the project seeds defaults or has registered hooks) lists the registered hooks and lets the user register a suggestion or their own phrase. Hooks live in the GLOBAL project_hooks table (slot API /api/project-hooks); phrases are unique APP-WIDE, so a duplicate/near-duplicate is refused (409) and shown as a toast. getHooks() (_lib/project-data.ts) provides the server-rendered initial list so the layer works with JS off; add/remove is progressive enhancement. Section headings + tooltips are localized via _data/tab-i18n.ts (slot language, English fallback).
seams.missing-keys: missing-keys-modal.client.tsx is a NATIVE, reusable part of the primitive (step 186.3): _data/required-keys.ts is generated at compose time from the project's declared integrations ({{PROJECT_INTEGRATIONS}}) into REQUIRED_ENV_KEYS; the modal, always mounted in _components/index.tsx, checks those keys on mount via GET /api/project-config/env?keys=… and, for any absent one, prompts the user to supply it — Save POSTs each key to the single-key slot setter (/api/project-config/env, step 186.4) which writes app/.env.local and restarts fractera-app. Dismissible; re-checks on the next open. Zero declared integrations → the modal renders nothing. Culture: an automation never silently lacks its keys — it asks.

params.category: Existing projects category slug (automation | fractera-pages | personal | other). Fixed English identifiers.
params.project: Project slug, kebab-case English identifier — the folder name IS the registry (never localized, never renamed casually).
params.title: Human project name shown on the page.
params.purpose: Why the project exists (description block). Optional — placeholder text if omitted.
params.automation: What is automated. Optional — placeholder text if omitted.
params.how: How the automation works. Optional — placeholder text if omitted.
params.cron: true/false — the project will run cron processes. Recorded in the README declaration only; execution is the P6 cron infrastructure.
params.hooks: true/false — the project answers to spoken trigger phrases (hooks, step 187). Recorded in the README declaration; the composed project carries the Hooks layer (panel + table) and its phrases register through the global /api/project-hooks table.
params.integrations: JSON array [{ name, envKeys[] }] — external integrations with their env keys. Recorded in the README declaration; keys are materialized into app/.env.local via the env setter + rebuild when executing.
